claim: Gemini 1.5 Flash is the clear winner for parallel function calling: 100% accuracy on 15-tool chains, fastest execution (subsecond), and cheapest cost across 30+ test runs.
actionable: For agentic workflows requiring reliable parallel function calling, benchmark Gemini 1.5 Flash first—it outperforms Claude 3.5 Sonnet (27% accuracy on tool calls) and GPT-4o by significant margins.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: Claude 3.5 Sonnet's function calling mode achieves only 27% accuracy on 15-tool parallel chains; JSON-based prompting with the same model performs better than native function calling.
actionable: When using Anthropic models, test both native tool_use and JSON-structured prompting—for parallel function calling, JSON prompting may outperform function calling capabilities.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: GPT-4o fails at long chains of parallel tool calls (only 40% accuracy on 10-tool chains), contradicting expectations that flagship models are best for function calling.
actionable: Don't assume flagship models (GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet) are best for tool calling—run benchmarks on your specific use case; Google's Gemini models and smaller models may outperform.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: Smaller Gemini 1.5 Flash is faster and cheaper than larger reasoning models (o1-mini) for function calling tasks—speed and cost don't require reasoning architecture for this task.
actionable: For tool-calling-heavy agentic workflows, prefer non-reasoning models (Flash, Sonnet) over reasoning models (o1-mini)—reasoning overhead doesn't improve function calling performance.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: Manual JSON prompting (structured output requests) enables function calling for models without native tool_use support; this workaround often matches or beats native function calling.
actionable: For agentic systems, use structured JSON prompts for tool calls across all models—it's compatible with models without native function calling and often more reliable than tool_use APIs.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: Long-running agentic workflows demand every tool call succeed in sequence; 1-2 failures in a chain of 15 tools breaks the entire workflow, making accuracy (not speed) the primary metric.
actionable: When building multi-step agentic workflows, prioritize LLM accuracy on function calling over speed—one misplaced tool call cascades and breaks downstream tasks.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark

claim: Gemini Experimental is slower (10+ seconds vs 1-2 for Flash) but achieves 100% accuracy; it's a valid choice if latency isn't critical and accuracy/cost matter most.
actionable: For background agentic tasks (CI/CD, batch processing) where latency is uncritical, Gemini Experimental offers accuracy and cost advantages; for real-time tasks, use Flash.
source_title: Best LLM for Parallel Function Calling: 14 LLM, 420 Prompt, 1 Winner Benchmark
